hero.subtitle: Swiss Executive Chef & Culinary Consultant
hero.tagline1: Four decades of experience. Three continents. One passion:
hero.tagline2: Artisan cuisine from fresh, natural ingredients — from scratch.

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Chef by passion, entrepreneur by conviction
about.p1: I am a classically trained Swiss chef — apprenticed at the Hotel de la Paix in Lausanne, shaped by the large-scale kitchens of the Anaheim Hilton in California, and honed over 20 years as an entrepreneur in Colombia.
about.p2: In Cartagena I founded Restaurante Casa Suiza — Swiss fusion cuisine with daily fresh bread, Basler Läckerli with coffee, and Spätzle as the bestseller. Later I built a complete food manufacturing operation: cheese, yogurt, charcuterie, baked goods — all artisanal and from scratch.
about.p3: When the milk quality wasn't good enough, I bought my own Jersey cows — for higher protein and fat content. I roasted coffee over charcoal in the Sierra Nevada, smoked meats with avocado wood, and aged blue cheese in the Andes. Now I'm bringing this experience back to Switzerland.

highlights.camper: Own camper van — flexible across Switzerland

usp.label: Your Advantage
usp.text: No accommodation needed. I bring my own camper van and am flexibly deployable across all of Switzerland — for seasonal or temporary engagements, with zero logistics on your end.

services.title: My Services
services.subtitle: Consultant, concept developer, and executive chef in one.

items.title: Menu Development & Consulting
items.description: Seasonal menu concepts developed from scratch. Local, fresh, sustainable — from Alpine wild herbs to classic Swiss dishes.

items.title: Food Cost Optimization
items.description: With an MBA and 30 years of entrepreneurial experience, I analyze purchasing, portioning, and workflows — for maximum profitability without compromising quality.

items.title: Staff Training & Kitchen Organization
items.description: Establish efficient workflows, train kitchen staff, and eliminate stress situations through smart menu design — not manage them.

items.title: Pop-up Events & Catering
items.description: From Swiss Night events to Alpine pop-up dinners. Experience with large-scale events, corporate catering, and exclusive private dining.

items.title: Artisanal Product Development
items.description: Cheese, charcuterie, baked goods, yogurt, jams, sauces — all artisanal and from scratch. Product development to market readiness.

items.title: Relief Chef & Replacement
items.description: Available at short notice as an experienced executive chef for emergencies, seasonal reinforcement, or holiday cover. Rapid onboarding guaranteed.

approach.title: My Approach
approach.firstWeekText: I get my hands dirty — from the chef's office to the dishwashing station. I observe production and operational processes, analyze workflows, and learn the organization from the ground up.
approach.stressText: When stress situations occur excessively, workflows need to be optimized — not the staff. Often, smart menu adjustments are enough to sustainably reduce kitchen pressure while ensuring quality and consistency.
approach.profitNichesText: that others overlook: On a rainy day in the Alps with no guests, I produce granola energy bars for hikers or kombucha as a refreshment — additional revenue from existing resources.
approach.menuDevText: Local first, fresh first, seasonal first, quality first. I hike through forests and Alpine meadows, gathering wild berries and mountain thyme for my kitchen.

experience.title: My Career
experience.subtitle: From Lausanne's hotel kitchens via California and Colombia — back to Switzerland.

items.title: Chef Apprenticeship — Hotel de la Paix, Lausanne
items.description: Three-year apprenticeship with Swiss Federal Certificate (Koch EFZ). Practice in fine dining and international cuisine. Additionally: Certificat d'Économie, École Lémania (1986).

items.title: Cook — Swiss Army
items.description: Preparing meals under military conditions. Focus on efficiency, logistics, and quality in large-scale catering.

items.title: Cook & Beverage Supervisor — Anaheim Hilton & Towers, California
items.description: Largest hotel in Southern California: 1,600 rooms, up to 8,000 guests daily. Worked across all kitchen departments (French, Italian, Mexican). Promoted to Beverage Supervisor: responsible for 5 bars/restaurants, 45 staff, USD 3.5M annual revenue.

items.title: BSc & MBA — University of Central Florida, Orlando
items.description: Bachelor of Science in Business Administration (May 1993, Summa Cum Laude, Top 2%, Scholarship) and Master of Business Administration (December 1994). Focus: Finance, Management, Marketing, Financial Analysis. Concurrent: Internships at Accent Consulting Group and U.S. Small Business Administration — including business plans for restaurant ventures.

items.title: Assistant Manager — Taco Bell (PepsiCo), Orlando
items.description: Managing a fast-food operation with USD 750,000 annual revenue. Responsible for staff management, inventory control, and customer relations.

items.title: General Manager & Product Development — Florida
items.description: Newquest Ltd. (1995–1996): Managing an internet startup — finance, IT, marketing. American Executive Centers (1996–1998): Development and launch of a multi-million dollar internet project, supporting the CFO with financial reporting and stock issuance.

items.title: Harvest Soaps — Own Business, Florida
items.description: Founded and managed a family business producing artisanal natural soaps with e-commerce distribution. Responsible for business planning, production, finance, marketing, and online sales. Built a brand from scratch.

items.title: MinCafé — Coffee Roastery, Minca (Sierra Nevada, Colombia)
items.description: Arrived in Colombia via a kitesurfing school in Cartagena. Developed a specialty coffee brand with innovative charcoal roasting in cooperation with local farmers. Additionally: mango vinegar, dried fruits, spirits. Pioneer work in a region controlled by paramilitary forces.

items.title: Casa Suiza — Own Restaurant, Cartagena
items.description: Founded and managed a Swiss fusion restaurant. Spätzle in all variations as bestseller, daily fresh bread with herb butter, Basler Läckerli and Magenbrot with coffee. Swiss quality for Colombian and international guests. Pivoted to food production for other hospitality businesses.

items.title: Artisanal Food Manufacturing — Medellín & Guarne
items.description: Production of cheese (Gouda, Feta, Brie, Blue, Halloumi, Belper Knollen, Mozzarella, Provolone), baked goods, smoked meats with avocado wood, yogurt, and jams. From 2018: Own dairy farm with Jersey cows for optimal milk quality. Primary supplier of feta and Greek yogurt to Greek restaurants in Medellín.

items.title: Return to Switzerland
items.description: Ready for new challenges. Available from August 2026 for seasonal and temporary engagements across all of Switzerland — with own camper van, no accommodation needed.

gallery.title: My Creations
gallery.subtitle: From artisan cheese to Black Forest cake to tricolor Spätzle — everything from scratch.

credentials.title: Qualifications & References
credentials.subtitle: Swiss federal qualifications, international diplomas, and top-tier references.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Let's discuss your requirements — no obligation, no fuss.
contact.location: All of Switzerland — also available worldwide
contact.locationText: Flexible with own camper van — no accommodation needed
benefits: No accommodation needed — own camper van
Ready to start immediately — fast onboarding
Chef Certificate + MBA = Culinary meets Business
Ideal for seasonal, relief, or consulting work
contact.ctaText: Whether seasonal reinforcement, consulting project, or emergency replacement — write to me and we'll find the right solution.

footer.tagline2: 40 years of experience — Three continents — One passion.
